metadata.description: Global AI regulatory compliance tracker with key milestones, penalties, and enforcement dates
metadata.source: Alpha One Index — AI TRiSM Market Index

regulations.name: EU AI Act
regulations.full_name: Regulation (EU) 2024/1689 of the European Parliament and of the Council — Artificial Intelligence Act

key_dates.description: Regulation entered into force; grace periods begin
Prohibition on unacceptable-risk AI systems applies (Article 5): social scoring, subliminal manipulation, real-time biometric surveillance in public spaces
General Purpose AI (GPAI) model rules apply, including obligations for providers of foundation models
AI literacy training obligations for AI providers and deployers take effect
High-risk AI systems in Annex III categories (HR, education, critical infrastructure, etc.) must comply with full transparency, data governance, and human oversight requirements
High-risk AI systems subject to existing EU product safety legislation must comply
Full Act enforcement including all remaining provisions

risk_classification.unacceptable_risk: Prohibited (e.g., social scoring, real-time biometric surveillance)
risk_classification.high_risk: Heavy obligations: conformity assessment, data governance, transparency, human oversight, cybersecurity
risk_classification.minimal_risk: No obligations (e.g., spam filters, AI-enabled video games)

regulations.name: NIST AI RMF
regulations.full_name: NIST Artificial Intelligence Risk Management Framework (AI RMF 1.0)

key_dates.description: NIST releases AI RMF 1.0 with GOVERN, MAP, MEASURE, MANAGE core functions
Companion Playbook providing implementation guidance
NIST AI 600-1: AI RMF generative AI profile addressing GenAI-specific risks

regulations.adoption: Widely adopted by US federal agencies; referenced in US Executive Orders; basis for many state AI laws

regulations.name: ISO/IEC 42001
regulations.full_name: ISO/IEC 42001:2023 — Information technology — Artificial intelligence — Management system

key_dates.description: First international standard for AI management systems; defines requirements for responsible AI development, deployment, and use
Certification bodies begin issuing ISO 42001 compliance certificates to organizations

regulations.scope: AI management system requirements for organizations developing, providing, or using AI products and services
key_clauses: Clause 6: Planning (AI risk and impact assessment)
regulations.annex_a_controls: 36 controls across AI policy, roles, resources, impact assessment, AI system lifecycle, data management, human oversight

regulations.name: US Executive Order 14110
regulations.full_name: Executive Order 14110 on the Safe, Secure, and Trustworthy Development and Use of Artificial Intelligence

key_dates.description: Biden Administration signs comprehensive AI executive order
NIST directed to develop AI safety and security standards; dual-use foundation model requirements
Trump Administration rescinds EO 14110; some agency guidance remains

regulations.name: US State AI Laws
regulations.full_name: US State-Level AI Legislation (Colorado, Illinois, Texas, New York, California, Connecticut, others)

key_dates.description: Colorado SB 205 signed — first US state comprehensive AI law; high-risk AI governance requirements effective February 1, 2026
Colorado AI Act enforcement begins for high-risk AI systems
Illinois AI Video Interview Act (2019) requires employer disclosure and bias testing for AI hiring tools
NYC LL144 requires bias audits for automated employment decision tools; in effect
Multiple California AI bills; Governor vetoed SB 1047 (foundation model safety) in September 2024; AB 2013 requires training data disclosures

key_jurisdictions.scope: High-risk AI systems; developer and deployer obligations
Automated employment decision tools; mandatory bias audits

regulations.name: China AI Regulations
regulations.full_name: China Generative AI Measures + CAC Algorithm Registration Requirements

key_dates.description: CAC rules on algorithmic recommendation services take effect
CAC rules on deep synthesis (deepfakes) take effect
CAC Interim Measures for the Management of Generative Artificial Intelligence Services take effect; content safety, transparency, and data requirements
GPAI providers must register foundation models with CAC; security assessments for public-facing services

key_provisions: Mandatory registration for public-facing generative AI services

regulations.name: UK AI Regulatory Framework
regulations.full_name: UK Pro-innovation Approach to AI Regulation + AI Safety Institute

key_dates.description: UK government publishes pro-innovation AI regulatory approach; principles-based rather than legislation
UK AI Safety Institute (AISI) launched; frontier AI safety evaluations
Bletchley AI Safety Summit; 16 AI companies sign voluntary safety commitments
UK releases AI Opportunities Action Plan; regulatory clarity for AI deployment

key_provisions: Sector-specific regulator approach (FCA, ICO, CMA each apply principles to their domain)
Five cross-sector AI principles: safety, security and robustness; transparency and explainability; fairness; accountability and governance; contestability and redress
Financial services AI: SS1/23 Dear CEO letter (model risk management)
ICO AI and data protection guidance

penalties.notes: Sector regulators enforce using existing powers (FCA, ICO, CMA etc.); ICO can fine up to £17.5M or 4% of global annual turnover

regulations.name: SR 11-7
regulations.full_name: Federal Reserve / OCC SR Letter 11-7: Guidance on Model Risk Management

key_dates.description: Fed/OCC guidance on model risk management; establishes MRM framework for banks
Regulators clarify SR 11-7 applies to AI/ML models used in credit decisions, AML, stress testing

penalties.notes: Banking regulators can impose MRAs (Matters Requiring Attention), cease-and-desist orders, civil money penalties

regulations.name: GDPR (AI Implications)
regulations.full_name: General Data Protection Regulation — AI and Automated Decision-Making Provisions

key_dates.description: GDPR becomes enforceable; Article 22 (automated decision-making) applies to AI
EDPB publishes Guidelines on AI and data protection

ai_specific_provisions: Article 22: Right not to be subject to solely automated decisions with significant effect; requires human oversight
Article 13-15: Transparency and explainability requirements for automated processing
Article 35: DPIA (Data Protection Impact Assessment) required for high-risk AI processing

regulations.name: HIPAA (AI in Healthcare)
regulations.full_name: Health Insurance Portability and Accountability Act — AI Applications in Healthcare

key_dates.description: HHS publishes AI strategy for health and human services

key_dates.milestone: FDA AI/ML Software as Medical Device guidance
key_dates.description: FDA updates guidance on AI/ML-based Software as a Medical Device (SaMD)

key_requirements: PHI protection in AI training data
Business Associate Agreements for AI vendors processing PHI
Risk analysis for AI systems handling PHI
FDA SaMD oversight for clinical AI tools

regulations.name: Canada AIDA
regulations.full_name: Canada Artificial Intelligence and Data Act (Bill C-27)

key_dates.description: Canada introduces Artificial Intelligence and Data Act as part of Bill C-27 (alongside Consumer Privacy Protection Act)
AIDA progresses to second reading in Parliament; significant revisions proposed
AIDA status uncertain following Canadian federal political changes; legislation may be reintroduced

key_provisions: High-impact AI system obligations: impact assessments, risk mitigation, monitoring, anomaly reporting
Prohibition on AI causing serious harm or biased outputs
Penalties up to 3% of global revenue for non-compliance

penalties.notes: Penalties for non-compliance with compliance orders; criminal penalties for reckless harm

frameworks_voluntary.name: OECD AI Principles
frameworks_voluntary.description: First intergovernmental standard on AI; five principles: inclusive growth, human-centred values, transparency, robustness, accountability

frameworks_voluntary.name: IEEE Ethically Aligned Design
frameworks_voluntary.description: Framework for ethical AI design covering autonomous and intelligent systems

frameworks_voluntary.name: G7 Hiroshima AI Process
frameworks_voluntary.description: Guiding principles and code of conduct for AI developers; voluntary international alignment
